H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78 — strongest HEALING overstimulation protocol. AutoGluon aligns at 6.859 (+0.29 observed). High YT views (30) despite NULL retention.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: A standout protocol for overstimulation, helping listeners calm down. The music is well-loved, though few people know it exists.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73 — adequate performance. AutoGluon predicts 6.92 (no delta). Low YT reach (views=5).
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Works well for overwhelming moments, especially for those relaxing or commuting. It’s soothing but not widely discovered.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68 — late entrainment (5-10 mins) consistent with longer DESCENT. AutoGluon predicts 6.92 (+0.12 observed).
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Relaxes those who feel overwhelmed, particularly before sleeping. It settles in quickly but few have tried it.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93 — strongest confidence in HEALING anxiety protocols. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 (unrealized potential +0.62). Qualitative settlement within 10 minutes aligns with DESCENT design. YouTube discovery gap (views=7) despite high efficacy.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This protocol helps ease anxiety, especially in very stressful moments. Most listeners feel calmer within 10 minutes, though almost no one has discovered it yet.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62 — meaningful performance with caregiver skew (6/20 clinical). AutoGluon predicts 6.94 (no delta). Slow discovery (views=6) mirrors low YT reach.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Reliable for anxiety, though some listeners don’t connect deeply. It starts working quickly during high-stress moments.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89 — divergence in settlement times (10 mins) vs peer protocols. AutoGluon misalignment (7.08 obs vs 6.20 predicted) — possible subgroup effect.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Works well for anxiety but takes a little longer to settle into. It’s gentle and calming for those who benefit.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70 — crisis baseline response as expected. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (gap +1.60) — investigate entrainment delay.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Designed for crisis moments, it provides immediate relief though from a difficult starting point. The music and rhythm are mostly well-received.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70 — moderate crisis response. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (unmet potential +1.43). Qualitative split (neutral vs loved).
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Helps in crisis, especially for calming quickly. Listeners have mixed reactions to the music, but the rhythm is grounding.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91 — replicate pattern with small-n instability. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 (unmet potential +1.43).
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Effective for intense stress, though some listeners struggle with the rhythm. It starts working quickly for most.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86 — sleep protocol benchmark. AutoGluon predicts 7.539 (observed +0.35). YT reach gap (views=8).
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: A reliable sleep aid, used successfully before bedtime. It quiets the mind, though most haven’t encountered it.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55 — clinical population skew (6/15). AutoGluon predicts 7.539 (gap +0.43). Negative rhythm opinions (3/15 busy).
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Helps with sleep, particularly for those being cared for. The rhythm feels busy to some listeners, though it generally works.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68 — small-n stability. AutoGluon predicts 7.539 (gap +0.47).
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Supports rest, often used in self-care routines. It settles in within 5-10 minutes, but discovery remains low.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82 — focus protocol variance. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (gap +1.27). YT reach=5.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus support with mixed reactions. It helps during work or study sessions, though not widely used.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60 — moderate success. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (gap +0.54).
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Improves clarity, especially in caregiving settings. The rhythm splits opinion, but it generally aids focus.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97 — top THRIVING protocol. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (observed +0.10). YT views=16.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Top focus protocol, used successfully in complex tasks. It’s loved by those who try it, though hidden from most.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73 — creativity baseline. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (gap +0.99). Low YT reach (views=2).
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Boosts creativity during focused tasks, though response varies. It’s used in low-pressure moments and hasn’t been widely shared.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41 — adequate. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (gap +0.94).
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Spark creativity, especially for new projects. The music is loved, but it’s largely undiscovered.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32 — emerging standout. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (observed +0.45). Discovery gap (views=7).
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Highly effective for creativity, though few have tried it. It’s best for sustained creative work once discovered.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34 — balance maintenance. AutoGluon predicts 7.693 (observed +0.65). YT views=3.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Maintains balance during self-care. It’s used in therapy sessions, though few have encountered it.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03 — unstable (std>2). AutoGluon predicts 7.693 (gap +1.01).
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Stabilizes moods, though listener responses vary. It works best when used consistently over time.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73 — developing. AutoGluon predicts 7.693 (gap +0.76).
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Aids balance, though underexplored. It’s used in study sessions and caregiving, with more potential.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94 — mood baseline. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 (gap +1.49).
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Lifts mood during active tasks, used in recovery moments. It’s well-liked, though listeners don’t discover it.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71 — adequate. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 (gap +1.47).
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Improves outlook, particularly after crises. The music is soothing, though reach remains limited.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74 — developing. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 (gap +1.95).
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results — some find it uplifting, others indifferent. It’s less consistent than other mood protocols.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01 — motivation variability. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 (gap +1.52).
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivates slowly, used in productivity settings. It helps some restart tasks, though response varies.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63 — low ASCENT response. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 (gap +1.67).
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Supports drive, though listeners take time to benefit. It’s used in study sessions, with mixed results.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38 — ASCENT success. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 (gap +0.73).
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Effectively boosts energy, especially in challenging moments. It works well for those who complete it.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62 — mind clarity baseline. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 (gap +0.79).
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Clarifies the mind, used in self-reflection. The music is well-liked, though it hasn’t been widely tested.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64 — crate. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 (gap +0.73).
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Strong clarity in quiet moments, though response varies. It’s loved by those who try it, but few have done so.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45 — critical outlier. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 (gap +1.84). Catastrophic responses require intervention.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Needs refinement — some listeners find it ineffective or even unsettling. It’s less reliable than other clarity protocols.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The three series demonstrate distinct response profiles consistent with design intent, though notable anomalies and opportunities for refinement emerge across protocols.
• HEALING protocols show strongest overall performance with lower variance (std=1.77), particularly in anxiety and over-stimulation contexts.
• THRIVING series exhibits variable efficacy with standout performers like T-FC-L3 (mean=7.63), but generally lower listener engagement.
• TRANSFORMING protocols underperform relative to predictions, with key exceptions like X-MD-L3 (mean=7.15), suggesting ascension design challenges.
• YouTube reach remains universally low (views < 50 for most), hindering discoverability of high-efficacy protocols like H-OS-L1 (mean=7.15).
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series-level patterns align with mechanistic design, though subgroup effects and engagement gaps warrant investigation.
• HEALING: Descending arcs achieve consistent entrainment (mean=6.98), particularly effective for crisis (H-CR-L1) and sleep (H-SL-L1) contexts.
• THRIVING: Hold phases optimize cognitive balance (mean=6.96), peaking in Focus & Clarity (T-FC-L3), but lower adoption limits confidence in generalization.
• TRANSFORMING: Ascending arcs show variable responses (mean=6.59), with motivation (X-MD-L3) outperforming clarity (X-MC-L3) — longer protocols exhibit delayed but stronger entrainment.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Key protocol-level irregularities require prioritized attention.
• X-MC-L3 (mean=5.7, std=2.45): Extreme underperformance warrants immediate design review — violates ASCENT intent with catastrophic responses (20% worsened state).
• H-SL-L2 (mean=7.09): Clinician-driven outlier with neutral music reception (6/15 neutral) and slow settle (10 mins) — possible mismatch between protocol design and clinical use case.
• T-CF-L3 (mean=7.28, n=18): Undiscovered potential (16 YouTube views) — high predictedscore (7.728) not yet reaching audience.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements address identified gaps and opportunities.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3: Develop L2 variant with revised Hz trajectory after expert review of catastrophic responses (n=10).
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-CR-L1/H-CR-L2/H-CR-L3: Audit crisis protocol gradients for ceiling effects — consider post-intervention stabilization holds.
• T-FC-L3: Optimize throttling algorithm to prioritize�� protocol in THRIVING recommendations given high efficacy and low visibility.
• X-MD-L3: Study motivational response curves — longer ASCENT duration correlates with improved outcomes despite protocol design challenges.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Analytical stability and clustering patterns reveal evolving framework interpretation.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 27/30 protocols show sim < 0.85 (notable drift), e.g., H-SL-L1 (0.701) and T-FC-L1 (0.718) — unstable analytics from small-n volatility.
• SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Crisis/HEALING protocols form tight clusters (H-CR-L1<>L2: 0.863) distinct from isolated outliers like H-SL-L2 (mean_sim=0.662) — indicative of divergent listener demographics.
• Confidence: Drift and clustering insights remain exploratory until n > 50 per protocol.
• YT INTEGRATION: Null retention data precludes validation of model predictions — urgent need for aggregated view_duration metrics.
